item.title: Hack Assembler
item.description: Full code can be found at github. It had been quite some time since I started off the Nand2Tetris Part I course on coursera. Because I wasn’t paying for it I wasn’t under the pressure of finishing the assignments but I still wanted to do it anyways but the last week’s assignment was kind of a daunting task for some reason. They ask you to build an assembler for the hack assembly, which the computer that you build throughout the course utilizes, and they also provide a complete breakdown of the logic, design, and tips on how to approach it.

item.title: Train to Pakistan
item.description: Author: Khushwant Singh The abilit of us humans to unite is an amazing sight to behold, but our ability to separate, or rather unite against, is equally terrifying. Author guides us through the events of a village that was torn apart following the India-Pakistan split. The people in the village, even though not wanting anything to do with the seperation, people that just wanted peace, were forced into submission by the two governing bodies; bodies that forced them against their wants, in the name of helping them.

item.title: Password Generator: Backend
item.description: For quite a while now, I wanted to learn Rust. I tried the online book, but I haven’t finished it till now, instead, after learning what I thought to be enough basics, I recreated my old terminal program into this web-based API just to see if I can use it or not. Rust actually provides a code snippet for this in their cookbook. There is even a crate for this exact purpose, and their code snippet is far better than mine.

item.title: about
item.description: Hey There! I’m Rena. Rena isn’t my actual name, though. I am really interested in the theory and foundations of computer systems. I enjoy exploring new topics and learning more as I go through my life. Besides CS, I am also interested in photography and reading books.\ As time goes on, this website will grow alongside me. So, maybe check in after a few years or so. Perhaps I will have more interesting things to show you then…
